root_text.sn35.159:1.1: “Rūpe, bhikkhave, yoniso manasi karotha, rūpāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassatha.
root_text.sn35.159:1.2: Rūpe, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yoniso manasikaronto, rūpāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassanto rūpesupi nibbindati.
root_text.sn35.159:1.10: dhamme, bhikkhave, yoniso manasi karotha, dhammāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassatha.
root_text.sn35.159:1.11: Dhamme, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yoniso manasikaronto, dhammāniccatañca yathābhūtaṁ samanupassanto dhammesupi nibbindati.

translation_text.sn35.159:0.3: Focus, the Exterior, and the End of Relishing
translation_text.sn35.159:1.1: “Mendicants, rationally apply the mind to sights. Truly see the impermanence of sights.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.2: When a mendicant does this, they grow disillusioned with sights.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.3: When relishing ends, greed ends. When greed ends, relishing ends.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.5: When relishing and greed end, the mind is said to be well freed.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.6: Rationally apply the mind to sounds …
translation_text.sn35.159:1.10: ideas. Truly see the impermanence of ideas.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.11: When a mendicant does this, they grow disillusioned with ideas.
translation_text.sn35.159:1.14: When relishing and greed end, the mind is said to be well freed.”
